title: Polystichopsis lurida
intro: Polystichopsis lurida, also known as Lurid Lady Fern, is a type of fern plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, Polystichopsis lurida has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Polystichopsis lurida is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal capsules and tinctures.
taxonomy: Polystichopsis lurida is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is part of the larger group known as the wood ferns or Dryopteridaceae. The wood ferns belong to the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The division Pteridophyta is further classified as part of the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Polystichopsis lurida reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.

The plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds and reduce swelling.
